methods.description: Process for collecting peat samples from various sites using a Hiller peat sampler, ensuring stability and proper sample handling for subsequent analysis.

steps.description: A Hiller peat sampler was used to collect samples at intervals of 10 centimeters.
steps.searchable_summary: use Hiller peat sampler to collect samples at 10 cm intervals

steps.description: At Crusoe Lake, the sampling was done through about 0.5 meters of water from a floating platform constructed by lashing two boats together with two-by-fours.
steps.searchable_summary: construct floating platform for sampling in water

steps.description: In removing the peat from the chamber of the sampler, the surface peat was scraped away and a few grams for analysis taken from inside the core.
steps.searchable_summary: scrape surface peat and collect core sample for analysis

steps.description: Each sample was wrapped in aluminum foil and placed in a pint polyethylene bag with a label inside.
steps.searchable_summary: wrap samples in aluminum foil and label

steps.description: Later, in the laboratory, these samples were transferred to labeled glass vials and stored in 30 percent alcohol.
steps.searchable_summary: transfer samples to glass vials and store in alcohol

methods.description: Procedure for processing peat samples with high mineral content and analyzing pollen content using chemical treatments and microscopy.

steps.description: Those samples with a high mineral content were placed in HF for 24 hours, followed by heating to the boiling point.
steps.searchable_summary: treat high mineral content samples with HF and heat

steps.description: After rinsing in HCl and distilled water, the sediments were boiled in 5 percent KOH and acetolyzed.
steps.searchable_summary: rinse samples in HCl, boil in KOH, and acetolyze

steps.description: The residue was mounted in glycerin jelly prestained with either crystal violet or basic fuchsin.
steps.searchable_summary: mount residue in prestained glycerin jelly

steps.description: A minimum of 150 tree pollens were counted, except where a scarcity of pollen on the slide made this impossible.
steps.searchable_summary: count minimum of 150 tree pollens using microscope

steps.description: The count was made using a binocular microscope with a calibrated mechanical stage.
steps.searchable_summary: use binocular microscope for pollen count
